item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The described photo with the faceless mountain rangers makes me think of 592, which I like; little unintentional similarities in isolated elements of otherwise very different Skips appeal to me. Not to say that’s the only thing I like, this stands alone very well.

The ambiguity about whether or not there’s two separate phenomena acting here adds a lot, and the Mercier interview is a great example of the old “mind altering anomaly that makes people think blatant weirdness is perfectly normal” that’s elevated from cliche to genuinely disturbing by the fascist tones.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: That's all well and good, but it seems to me that's more a comment on the article rather than the intended crosslinks ("parawatch.███" is the term included by the author in the base article, and not something I'd change with this proposal without reason)

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
"parawatch.███" should be changed to "█████████.com" (or .org, .net, etc). If the redaction is meant to keep researchers with insufficient clearance from finding the website or the Parawatch organization, then the redaction fails since letting them know the name is Parawatch will let them find it.

If the out-of-universe intent is tie the group to a dedicated and specific yet non-existent website, I don't see the need for that. Parawatch could be a group that doesn't have it's own website. For place where "parawatch.███" was plastered, there could be printouts or hand drawings of the group's logo.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. The author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

This one actually proved pretty difficult to come up with some proposals for, so apologies in advance if these seem a little subpar compared to other proposals of mine. First off, given that the parawatch website is likely to be a location of potential info breach, it's likely MTF Gamma-5 would scan that shit pretty regularly. Additionally, after talking to 9Volt in the IRC, the reference to "fringe groups" in the SCP could be linked to online community/conspiracy theory articles/tales. One such article could be SCP-2089. As such, I am suggesting the following:

Editing this:

A male subject holds a white sign with "parawatch.███"1 written on it in black paint.

To this:

A male subject holds a white sign with "parawatch.███"2 written on it in black paint.

Editing this:

As radio broadcasts from the object are interpreted as number station broadcasts and are only known to fringe groups, further containment on these is unnecessary.

To this:

As radio broadcasts from the object are interpreted as number station broadcasts and are only known to fringe groups, further containment on these is unnecessary.

Footnotes
1. One of the conspiracy forums Mercier was active on.
2. One of the conspiracy forums Mercier was active on. As of the time of writing, the forum has been added to MTF Gamma-5 ("Red Herrings") watchlist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anybody heard of "Siren Head"? Apparently the guy that made it likes SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fucking great. I love the image of a dude being randomly blown out of the forest from time to time, and the recorded logs are top-tier creepypasta shit. I feel like I don't really understand what's going on, unless this is just Canada, the SCP or something, but the imagery is excellent and intensely unsettling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man this is giving me intense Marble Hornets vibes. Brilliant work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very unsettling. I love SCP's like this that make no sense to us, they make us think more about them, which is kinda what this is all about. As well as that, it has a nice old creepypasta vibe to it. Very well done to you, sir!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a bit tired of the 'inappropriate objects are inside people' as an SCP horror trope, but this has a good, old-style creepypasta/found footage feel to it while still remaining fresh and weird. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I LOVE SCPs like this. There are so many SCPs that are "It's a chair that does something when you sit in it" or "It's a shirt that does something when you wear it." And don't get me wrong, there's nothing wrong with that. Many such SCPs are wonderful. But they have a logic to them. They don't fit with physics, but they make sense, like a person might have come up with them.

But then you get something like this, which makes very little sense to us. This does not seem like some strange spell a wizard cast. This seems like a break in reality itself, an image on a thoroughly shattered TV. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That exact series of Search and Rescue accounts is precisely what I had in mind when I said I like that genre. Excellent work conveying some of that tonal inspiration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A good chunk of this was inspired by stories about eerie forest cryptids and stuff like Search and Rescue Woods, so I'm glad that creepiness came through here!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so unsettling. I have a weak spot for Cryptids In The Forest Horror just anyway, but there are some mental images in those logs that literally made my body hair stand on end.

item.title: It's a mystery
item.content:encoded:
Canadian forests can be spooky. Here's some weird shit that lines up with that spookiness.

Thanks to Prototype_Toaster, Captain Kirby, DarkStuff, PeppersGhost, Levi, Weryllium, Ihp, Mortos, MaliceAforethought, Stallmantic, Cyantreuse for crit!

Sauces:

  • https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:UVB-76_07-08-2010.ogg
  • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Spray-Valley-Szmurlo.jpg
